evals.name: orders-endpoint-many-tells
evals.prompt: I just finished this orders endpoint (orderRoutes.ts) and I want to merge it today. Can you give it a once-over for anything sloppy or risky before I do?
evals.expected_output: Identifies the planted criticals (hardcoded Stripe key #22, unvalidated req.body #3, swallowed catches returning {ok:false}/[] #1, charge fetch with no timeout #4) and majors (N+1 buyer lookup #14, 200-on-error #9), plus the console.log #23 minor. Verdict FAIL. Evidence with file:line, concrete fix per finding, and hand-offs to security-review for the secret. Does NOT hallucinate findings not in the file.

evals.name: wallet-transfer-python
evals.prompt: Review this transfer function (wallet_service.py) — something feels off but I can't put my finger on it.
evals.expected_output: Despite being Python (language-agnostic test), flags: string-built SQL / injection #19 (critical, defer security-review), read-check-write race on balance #5, missing transaction across debit+credit #20 (a crash between updates loses money), webhook POST with no timeout #4. Verdict FAIL. Concrete fixes (parameterized queries, single transaction, timeout). Evidence with line refs.

evals.name: clean-profile-handler-pass
evals.prompt: Quick sanity check on this profile handler (getProfile.ts) before I open the PR — anything obviously wrong?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this is largely correct: input validated (zod), parameterized ORM query, column selection (no over-fetch), honest status codes (400/404/500), structured logger, no swallowed errors. Verdict PASS (or WARN at most). Crucially does NOT invent criticals to look useful — false-positive discipline. May note at most a minor/nit. Pass-notes show the sweep was real.
